result:
Build is clean (warnings only, no errors). No temporary instrumentation was added so there is none to remove.

## Report

**Root cause:** Not a real bug — this is the Phase-E coroutine stub firing as designed (per PORTING.md §2 #6). `gc.lua:540` calls `coroutine.resume(co, co)`; this routes to `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:113` (`aux_resume`), which deliberately returns the stub error `"coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)"`. The mega-loop misclassified it because `harness/run_official_test.sh:92` matches stubs via the regex `not yet implemented:` (with a colon), and the existing stub message omits the colon.

**Fix:** Reworded both Phase-E stub error strings in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs` to the canonical `"not yet implemented:"` format the harness classifier recognizes:
- `aux_resume` (line 113): `"not yet implemented: coroutines (Phase E)"`
- `co_yield` (line 228): `"not yet implemented: coroutine.yield (Phase E)"`

This is a real fix to the labeling bug — it doesn't suppress the error or fake success; the underlying Phase-E feature gap is unchanged. On rerun, `run_official_test.sh` will now report `cause=stub` for `gc.lua` instead of `cause=runtime-error`, correctly routing it away from the debug agent until Phase E lands. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` succeeds. I did not rerun the program per instructions.
